rationale: Chen and Lurie (2013) is cited in the theory section to justify a core mechanism in the profanity paper's moderator argument: attribution of outcomes to product versus reviewer. This is not just a background list citation, because that attribution idea is used to build predictions about when swear words are diagnostic. The paper also cites Chen and Lurie when specifying field-study measurement/modeling choices for helpfulness and valence controls, indicating practical carryover of prior WOM methodology.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Chen and Lurie (2013)'s attribution-based online WOM logic, arguing that readers evaluate whether review outcomes are caused by the product versus the reviewer when interpreting swear-word cues. It also follows that prior work's review-helpfulness modeling conventions in its field analyses.
